mnemonics.title: Virtual Attribute Inheritance
mnemonics.content: VIRTUAL: V-irtual I-nheritance R-uns T-hrough A-ll R-elevant L-ayers - Once virtual, always virtual through the inheritance chain

mnemonics.title: Virtual Destructor Memory
mnemonics.content: DESTRUCTOR: D-elete E-nsure S-afe T-ype R-un O-ut T-hrough O-rder R-ight - Virtual ensures correct destruction order

mnemonics.title: V-Table Remember
mnemonics.content: V-TABLE: V-irtual T-ABLE - Think of it as a 'menu' where each entry is a function pointer to the actual implementation

acronyms.expansion: Virtual Function Table - A lookup table containing function pointers to virtual functions
acronyms.usage: Used by compiler for runtime polymorphism dispatch

acronyms.expansion: Virtual Pointer - Hidden pointer in each object pointing to class vtable
acronyms.usage: Enables runtime determination of which virtual function to call

acronyms.expansion: Run-Time Type Information - Mechanism to determine object type at runtime
acronyms.usage: Used by dynamic_cast and typeid operators

acronyms.expansion: Abstract method with no implementation (= 0 syntax)
acronyms.usage: Defines interface without implementation, makes class abstract

visualTips.visualization: Draw an arrow from object → vptr → vtable → function pointer → actual function code. This shows how runtime polymorphism works through indirection.
Create a vertical hierarchy: Base → Derived1 → Derived2. Mark virtual functions at each level with arrows showing override capability flows downward.
Draw a large rectangle (Derived) inside a smaller rectangle (Base). When assigned by value, only the Base portion remains, the Derived part gets 'sliced' off.
Timeline showing: [Base Constructor] → [Derived Constructor] → [Work] → [Derived Destructor] → [Base Destructor] - like building floors top-down, demolishing top-down.

analogies.analogy: A virtual function is like a restaurant menu - the interface (the dish name) is the same, but the actual food (implementation) depends on which restaurant (class) you go to.
The vtable is like a menu board in a fast-food chain. Each location has its own board, and your order (function call) is filled based on which board (vtable) the cashier (vptr) uses.
An abstract class is like an architectural blueprint - it defines what should be built but can't be built itself. You need a concrete implementation to actually create something.

analogies.concept: Virtual Destructor as Proper Cleanup Crew
analogies.analogy: A virtual destructor is like hiring a cleanup crew that knows how to properly dismantle everything, including all the specialized parts, not just the main structure.
